## One-Line Fibonacci Function

```python
fib = lambda n: n if n <= 1 else fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2)
```

### Usage
```python
print(fib(0))   # 0
print(fib(1))   # 1
print(fib(10))  # 55
print(fib(15))  # 610
```

### More Efficient Version (with memoization)
```python
from functools import lru_cache

fib = lru_cache(maxsize=None)(lambda n: n if n <= 1 else fib(n - 1) + fib(n - 2))
```

| Version | Time Complexity | Space Complexity |
|---------|----------------|-----------------|
| Basic | O(2ⁿ) | O(n) |
| Memoized | O(n) | O(n) |

The basic version is simple but **slow for large values** due to redundant recursive calls. The memoized version caches results, making it significantly faster.
